about.name: Anastasios Tilsizoglou
about.title: Senior Software Engineer in Test
about.description: Aspiring Test Solutions Architect and "Bridge Builder" with a Master’s in Applied Informatics. Expert in architecting scalable automation frameworks, streamlining enterprise QA processes, and integrating AI-driven protocols (MCP). Committed to translating technical rigor into business value.

experience.title: Senior Software Engineer in Test
responsibilities: Architected centralized infrastructure by transitioning from forked projects to a modular NPM node dependency model, standardizing automation across all organizational teams.
Designed custom quality dashboards with real-time data visualization, providing stakeholders with critical visibility for data-driven task prioritization.
Spearheaded AI innovation by deploying Model Context Protocols (MCPs) and AI skills to optimize technical context retrieval across engineering.
Engineered cost-efficient CI/CD pipelines using open-source frameworks, eliminating expensive licensing while maintaining high-velocity delivery.
Developed Jira synchronization scripts to instantly inform teams of CI/CD progress, bridging the gap between execution and status reporting.

experience.title: Software Engineer in Test
responsibilities: Integrated automated smoke and regression suites into daily CI/CD delivery cycles.
Designed custom tools for test execution, reducing manual overhead and expanding coverage to new product modules.
Influenced "Shift-Left" strategy by providing UX and performance feedback during early design phases.

experience.title: Software Engineer in Test
responsibilities: Executed Performance & Load Testing using LoadUI and Java scripts to resolve system bottlenecks.
Led code reviews as Primary Reviewer, ensuring automation scripts met strict architectural standards.
Authored comprehensive test artifacts and traceability matrices to ensure 100% requirement coverage.

experience.title: Android Developer
responsibilities: Uploaded Android applications to Google's Playstore.

articles.name: Core Design Patterns for Test Automation: From Chaos to Maintainable Frameworks
articles.summary: In software engineering, test automation is often compared to an assembly line in a factory. Each test is a "product" that must meet quality standards. But without proper structure, that assembly line quickly collapses into chaos: flaky tests, duplicated code, and runaway technical debt.

articles.name: Flaky Tests Driving You Crazy? Here's How to Fix and Prevent Them!
articles.summary: Discover proven strategies to identify, prevent, and fix flaky tests in your CI/CD pipeline — complete with real‑world insights and code examples.

articles.name: Unpopular Opinion (or Maybe Not): ISTQB Shouldn't Be for Testers — It Should Be for Everyone Else
articles.summary: This isn’t just another article about testing. It’s about respect — for the work, for the people who do it, and for the role it plays in delivering quality software.

articles.name: Unlock Effortless Updates: Mastering Maintainable Test Automation Frameworks
articles.summary: A guide to building test automation frameworks that are easy to update and scale, ensuring long-term viability and efficiency.

articles.name: More than a Year with Allure Reporting: From Reporting Tool to Team Catalyst
articles.summary: Leveraging Allure Reporting to Bridge Gaps Between Test Execution, CI/CD, and Team Collaboration.

articles.name: Manual Testing: The Foundation That Makes Test Automation Truly Work
articles.summary: Before we automate, we need to understand. Why manual testing and test analysis remain essential in building robust, meaningful automation.

articles.name: Assembly Lines & Algorithms: The Realities of Modern Engineering
articles.summary: A deep dive into how software engineering mirrors industrial production — exploring shared workflows, metrics, and a path forward beyond the digital factory.
